verses.text: 'I will stand upon my watch... and see what he will say'; God bids him write the vision plainly, that a runner may read it. Waiting on God. Faith watches for His answer and records it for others.
'I will stand upon my watch... and see what he will say'; God bids him write the vision plainly, that a runner may read it. Waiting on God. Faith watches for His answer and records it for others.
Though the vision tarry, wait for it; 'the just shall live by his faith.' The great word. Three times the New Testament builds the gospel on this: the righteous live by trusting God.
Though the vision tarry, wait for it; 'the just shall live by his faith.' The great word. Three times the New Testament builds the gospel on this: the righteous live by trusting God.
The proud man, restless as wine, enlarges his desire 'as hell,' never satisfied; all shall mock him. Greed unfilled. The covetous heart can never be content.
The proud man, restless as wine, enlarges his desire 'as hell,' never satisfied; all shall mock him. Greed unfilled. The covetous heart can never be content.
Those he spoiled shall rise and spoil him, for his bloodshed and violence. Just reversal. The plunderer is at last plundered.
Those he spoiled shall rise and spoil him, for his bloodshed and violence. Just reversal. The plunderer is at last plundered.
Woe to him who builds his nest on high by 'an evil covetousness'; 'the stone shall cry out of the wall.' Guilt exposed. Even the house built by sin testifies against its builder.
Woe to him who builds his nest on high by 'an evil covetousness'; 'the stone shall cry out of the wall.' Guilt exposed. Even the house built by sin testifies against its builder.
Woe to him who builds his nest on high by 'an evil covetousness'; 'the stone shall cry out of the wall.' Guilt exposed. Even the house built by sin testifies against its builder.

Woe to the city built with blood; men labour for vanity, but 'the earth shall be filled with the knowledge of the glory of the LORD.' A glorious hope. Beyond the ruin of the proud, God's glory will cover the earth.

Woe to the city built with blood; men labour for vanity, but 'the earth shall be filled with the knowledge of the glory of the LORD.' A glorious hope. Beyond the ruin of the proud, God's glory will cover the earth.

Woe to the city built with blood; men labour for vanity, but 'the earth shall be filled with the knowledge of the glory of the LORD.' A glorious hope. Beyond the ruin of the proud, God's glory will cover the earth.
Woe to him who makes his neighbour drunk to shame him; he shall be filled with shame himself. Cruelty repaid. The disgrace he dealt to others returns upon him.
Woe to him who makes his neighbour drunk to shame him; he shall be filled with shame himself. Cruelty repaid. The disgrace he dealt to others returns upon him.
Woe to him who makes his neighbour drunk to shame him; he shall be filled with shame himself. Cruelty repaid. The disgrace he dealt to others returns upon him.

What use is an idol, that men bid wood awake? 'But the LORD is in his holy temple: let all the earth keep silence before him.' Idols and the living God. Before the true God, the whole earth falls silent.
